title: Marital infidelity, abortion, PCP2
primary_topics: abortion and the constitutional moment of life
Vatican II / PCP 2 lay political engagement
sub_topics: specific intent presumption from fact of infidelity
Imbong v. Ochoa (April 9, 2014) — life begins at fertilization
Bernardo Villegas / Hilario Davide Jr. constitutional framing
PCP 2 Article 28 on lay political engagement
Acts and Decrees of PCP 2 (P308 St. Paul's Superstore)

stances.claim: The XXX v. People ruling is a rocky 9-5-1 example of the Court's liberal trek — it held that infidelity, 'however casual or infrequent,' constitutes psychological violence punishable under RA 9262, with the majority presuming criminal intent from 'the fact of infidelity itself.'

His simple advice to husbands: 'Infidelity, though casual or occasional, and whether with a willing mistress or a one-night stand, is dangerous to life and liberty.' RA 9262 punishes only philandering husbands; it is sub silentio on philandering wives.
stances.rhetorical_move: wry practical advisory + gender-asymmetry observation in the law

stances.claim: The Imbong v. Ochoa landmark 'definitively ruled that life begins at fertilization' — when sperm and egg unite — making any device, method, or drug that prevents the fertilized ovum from reaching the maternal womb constitutionally suspect; the Court rejected the implantation theory advocated by Hon. Edcel Lagman.

Per PCP 2 Article 28, lay men and women in responsible positions must 'help form the civic conscience of the voting population' and promote 'election to public office of leaders of true integrity,' while 'bishops, priests and religious must refrain from partisan politics… avoiding especially the use of the pulpit for partisan purposes.'
stances.rhetorical_move: verbatim ecclesial-document quotation as authority on lay-clergy political division of labor

institutions: Second Plenary Council of the Philippines
St. Paul's Superstore (San Antonio Village, Makati)
laws_treaties: 1987 Constitution Article II Section 12 (right to life)
Acts and Decrees of PCP 2 Article 28

signature_phrases: Infidelity, though casual or occasional, and whether with a willing mistress or a one-night stand, is dangerous to life and liberty
specific intent... may be conclusively presumed from the fact of infidelity itself
register_markers: THE CASE INVOLVES THE INTERPRETATION AND APPLICATION
THE VATICAN II AND PCP2 DOCUMENTS
decision_framework_signals: applies presumption-of-specific-intent doctrine to RA 9262 marital-infidelity cases
uses constitutional textualism plus framers' intent (Villegas/Davide) to anchor 'life begins at fertilization'
frames Catholic lay-clergy political division of labor (lay form civic conscience, clergy refrain from partisanship) as governance principle
cross_references: last Monday, I said the road to liberalism of our Supreme Court and the Catholic Church is rocky
